xcodebuild_test.details: Attempted in this environment and failed before executing tests because CoreSimulator/temp/result-bundle writes are not permitted here. Packet-provided evidence says the targeted tests passed.

xcodebuild_build.details: Attempted in this environment and failed during package-resolution/temp-file writes. Packet-provided evidence says build passed.

swiftlint.details: Repo-wide invocation exits nonzero in this environment and lints vendor/generated sources as well as project code.

tc_8_no_literal_hex_or_system_image_in_m.details: No `Color(red:`, `Color(hex:`, or `Image(systemName:` usage in the two reviewed molecule files.

validation_gates.details: No deprecated SwiftUI APIs found in `LSLocationContextBar.swift` or `LSRouteAttachmentCard.swift`.
Compact padding is expressed with raw literals and, more importantly, is not the actual rendered outer padding because `LSCard` adds its own padding first.
No async/loading state required for these static molecules.
No RED-phase evidence for this task was provided in the reviewed worktree.
Key tests inspect source strings/helpers instead of asserting rendered behavior, and they missed two spec regressions.
Swift generated tokens were edited directly, while the upstream icon manifest and other generated platforms remain unsynced.
No modular-design analysis artifact for this task was found.
No obvious Rule-of-2 extraction miss in the new molecule files.

critical_issues.title: Generated Swift token output was edited directly and is now out of sync with the token source of truth
critical_issues.problem: The file is explicitly marked generated-only, but `circle` and `circleFill` were added only in the Swift generated output. The upstream manifest `tokens/semantic/icons.json` still contains 31 icons and does not list either icon, while other generated platforms remain unchanged (`tokens/platforms/web/tokens.ts` and `tokens/platforms/kotlin/.../Tokens.kt` still expose the old icon set). The Swift file also still carries the old input hash.
critical_issues.fix: Add the icons to the token source of truth (`tokens/semantic/icons.json` plus SVG assets under `tokens/icons/` if needed), regenerate all platform outputs with the token generator, and commit the synchronized generated files. Do not hand-edit `Generated/Tokens.swift`.

high_issues.title: Compact padding acceptance criterion is not actually met on screen
high_issues.problem: The molecule wraps content in `LSCard(padding: compact ? .spacing3 : .spacing4)`, and `LSCard` adds its own outer padding (`spacing3` = 12, `spacing4` = 16) before the molecule adds `contentPadding`. In compact mode the effective outer padding is therefore 22 vertical / 24 horizontal, not the required 10 / 12.
high_issues.fix: Make the card's outer padding actually resolve to the specified compact spacing, either by allowing `LSCard` to render without built-in padding for this use case or by moving the exact compact padding responsibility entirely into one layer.

high_issues.title: Metrics row does not match the UC-MOL-08 spec
high_issues.problem: The spec/concept calls for mono distance + duration + 5-dot scenic meter + `SCENIC` label. The implementation instead renders distance, duration, elevation, and scenic dots, with no `SCENIC` label at all.
high_issues.fix: Remove the extra elevation metric from this molecule, add the required scenic label with the specified label typography, and update tests/stories to match the documented composition.

medium_issues.title: Tests still miss the real regressions that matter for this task
medium_issues.problem: The strengthened tests assert helper values and source-string presence, but they do not verify actual rendered compact padding after `LSCard` composition and they do not assert the required `SCENIC` label. That allowed both spec mismatches above to pass.
medium_issues.fix: Replace source-text assertions with behavior-focused tests around rendered composition/output contracts, and add coverage for effective compact padding and scenic label presence.

red_phase_evidence.details: No current-task RED evidence was found in the reviewed worktree.

tests_verify_behavior.details: Several tests validate source text or helper methods rather than end-user-visible behavior.

edge_cases_covered.details: Scenic icon clamping is covered, but effective compact layout and required scenic labeling are not.

consistency_findings: Route stripe token names map correctly for `.best`, `.alt1`, and `.alt2` in the Swift molecule.
Token pipeline consistency is broken because only Swift generated output was edited; Kotlin and web generated outputs did not receive the same icon additions.

visual_verification.details: Simulator-based visual verification was not possible in this environment because CoreSimulator/result-bundle writes are blocked.

verdict_rationale: The candidate fixes the prior star-vs-dot scenic meter regression, preserves the legacy molecule file, and adds the required stories/tests. However, it still fails the task at merge quality: the route card does not render the specified compact padding or metrics row, and the direct edit to generated Swift token output is a source-of-truth regression that will drift or be overwritten on the next token generation run.
